MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF QUI MING WANG'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Appearances: Sandy Sikavi of the Underwood Law Firm (415.574.5000) for plaintiff and moving party, present with paralegal Claire Brett; No other appearances.

The court has received a courtesy copy of plaintiff's FedEx receipt as requested. Discussed and the court rules as follows: denied without prejudice. Plaintiff is directed to follow up with department 301 on the status of their proposed order regarding the June 10, 2025, hearing. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF QI WANG's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Off calendar. Partition/Real Property law and motion matters to be heard in department 501 per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Visiting Judge: John M. True; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2025 Continued To Sep-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion, To Deem Matters Admitted, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF QI WANG's Motion To Deem Matters Admitted, And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is granted.

Plaintiff Qi Wang served requests for admission on defendant Weiwei Zhang. (Sikavi Dec. para. 6.) Zhang has not responded. (Sikavi Dec. para. 8.) Zhang failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by Zhang. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Wang also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions for three hours of attorney time plus filing fees, a reduced amount in light of the fact that no opposing brief was filed, eliminating the need for some time estimated in paragraph 9 of the Sikavi Declaration. The court orders Zhang to pay $1215 to Wang within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
